title: Pattern Usage Coverage — Compositions To Add
description: Worklist of catalog patterns that lack a real, documented usage in a shipping framework, application, or service. A pattern with zero documented uses is unproven in practice. Each item collects candidate frameworks/apps/services that implement the pattern, with verifiable upstream evidence (url + verbatim quote). Items graduate by having a verified usage added to a compositions-src/ shard, after which this worklist is regenerated and the item drops off. Generated by _build_compositions_todo.py; candidate research is hand-added and preserved across regenerations.

candidates.note: Inseq is a shipping Python feature-attribution library for generative LLMs that produces per-token attribution maps, including attention-based attribution extracting attention weights across all transformer layers and heads as the per-token relevance score; it surfaces relevance via weight extraction rather than ablation-perturbation strictly.

evidence.title: Attention Attribution — Inseq Documentation
evidence.quote: Attention weights attribution method. Attribution method that returns the attention weights of the model as attribution scores. The attention weights are averaged across all layers and heads, or a single layer can be selected.

evidence.title: inseq-team/inseq — Interpretability for Sequence Generation Models
evidence.quote: Inseq is a Pytorch-based hackable toolkit to democratize the access to common post-hoc interpretability analyses of sequence generation models.

candidates.note: Inter-agent connections in the agent graph are optimized by pruning or creating edges at run time, reshaping the collaboration topology instead of using a fixed structure.

evidence.title: metauto-ai/GPTSwarm README
evidence.quote: Notice that within an agent, the edges are fixed, whereas the inter-agent connections are getting optimized towards either edge pruning (value 0, blue) or creation (value 1, red).

candidates.note: A centralized orchestrator trained with reinforcement learning adaptively sequences and prioritizes a pool of LLM agents per task state, learning the collaboration topology end-to-end.

evidence.title: Multi-Agent Collaboration via Evolving Orchestration (arXiv:2505.19591)
evidence.quote: This orchestrator is trained via reinforcement learning to adaptively sequence and prioritize agents, enabling flexible and evolvable collective reasoning.

candidates.note: A Python library for programming LLM agents as behavior trees: LLM action leaves (CausalLMAction etc.) return success/failure and are composed by control nodes (sequence, fallback/selector, decorators).

evidence.title: RichardKelley/dendron
evidence.quote: Dendron is a library for building software agents using behavior trees and language models.

evidence.title: Dendron documentation (node types)
evidence.quote: behavior trees make it easy to compose simple behaviors (pieces of code) into collections of complex behaviors. [Control nodes include Sequence and Fallback (Selector); action types include CausalLMAction, ImageLMAction, LogLikelihoodAction.]

candidates.note: An orchestrator (lead) agent develops a strategy and decomposes the query into subtasks; inner subagents run their own loops in parallel, and the lead synthesizes results and decides whether to spawn more or refine the plan.

evidence.title: How we built our multi-agent research system
evidence.quote: When a user submits a query, the lead agent analyzes it, develops a strategy, and spawns subagents to explore different aspects simultaneously. ... The LeadResearcher synthesizes these results and decides whether more research is needed—if so, it can create additional subagents or refine its strategy.

candidates.vendor: ETH Zurich SPCL (Scalable Parallel Computing Lab)
candidates.note: Installable (pip) framework that models reasoning as a Graph of Operations executed by an LLM, letting thoughts be combined and refined across branches and generalizing CoT/ToT as special cases of the graph.

evidence.title: spcl/graph-of-thoughts — README
evidence.quote: This framework gives you the ability to solve complex problems by modeling them as a Graph of Operations (GoO), which is automatically executed with a Large Language Model (LLM) as the engine.

evidence.title: spcl/graph-of-thoughts — README
evidence.quote: allowing you to not only solve problems using the new GoT approach, but also to implement GoOs resembling previous approaches like CoT or ToT

candidates.note: Plug-and-play inference library that offloads a long prompt as a variable in a REPL/sandbox the LM navigates via code, launching recursive sub-LM calls over snippets so it answers over near-infinite-length contexts.

evidence.title: alexzhang13/rlm — README
evidence.quote: RLMs offload the context as a variable in a REPL environment that the LM can interact with and launch sub-LM calls inside of

evidence.title: alexzhang13/rlm — README
evidence.quote: enabling the LM to programmatically examine, decompose, and recursively call itself over its input

candidates.note: Builds an executable skill graph offline from SKILL.md packages, then at task time returns a bounded dependency-aware skill bundle (relevant skills plus their prerequisites) via a Claude Code MCP plugin.

evidence.title: Graph-of-Skills: Dependency-Aware Structural Retrieval for Massive Agent Skills
evidence.quote: GoS constructs an executable skill graph offline from skill packages, then at inference time retrieves a bounded, dependency-aware skill bundle through hybrid semantic-lexical seeding, reverse-aware Personalized PageRank, and context-budgeted hydration.

evidence.title: GitHub - davidliuk/graph-of-skills
evidence.quote: retrieves a small, ranked set of relevant skills at task time...along with their prerequisites and related capabilities

candidates.note: GenAI Data Fusion synthesises a query, executes it against internal databases and business applications via its Micro-Database to fetch structured data, then has the LLM generate the grounded answer.

evidence.title: What is Table-Augmented Generation (TAG)? | K2view
evidence.quote: The retrieval model queries your internal databases and business applications for structured data.

candidates.note: The user signs an open mandate that captures spending constraints (budget, allowed instruments) up front, letting the agent execute payments autonomously within those bounds and settle a specific charged amount on checkout.

evidence.title: Agent Payments Protocol (AP2) Documentation
evidence.quote: Open: Captures the user's constraints on payment (e.g., budget, allowed instruments) for autonomous execution.

evidence.title: Agent Payments Protocol (AP2) Documentation
evidence.quote: Captures the user's (or agent's) authorization for a specific transaction amount bound to a finalized checkout.

candidates.note: x402 lets an agent pay programmatically per HTTP request over the 402 Payment Required flow, so an agent can stream many autonomous micropayments for service access without per-transaction human approval.

evidence.title: x402 Overview - Coinbase Developer Documentation
evidence.quote: Buyers: Human developers and AI agents seeking to access paid services without accounts or manual payment flows.

evidence.title: x402 Overview - Coinbase Developer Documentation
evidence.quote: Let AI agents pay and access services autonomously with no keys or human input needed.

candidates.note: A static-analysis scanning engine based on information-flow analysis runs over the agent's execution traces to flag bugs such as looping behavior and threats such as prompt injections and unsafe code execution.

evidence.title: invariantlabs-ai/invariant README (Analyzer)
evidence.quote: The analyzer scans an agent's execution traces to identify bugs (e.g., looping behavior) and threats (e.g., data leaks, prompt injections, and unsafe code execution).

candidates.note: AG-UI is one event-streaming channel carrying both directions: the frontend POSTs user prompts/state into the agent and listens to the same SSE stream for the agent's text, tool calls and state deltas; session/cancellation events let the agent pause mid-run to ask the user for input.

evidence.title: AG-UI Protocol
evidence.quote: AG-UI (Agent–User Interaction) is the general-purpose, bi-directional connection between a user-facing application and any agentic backend.

candidates.note: LangGraph streams the agent's tokens and intermediate state to the user as the graph runs (messages/updates/values modes) while its interrupt / human-in-the-loop mechanism lets a running graph pause and accept a user impulse back in, giving a two-way exchange over one run.

evidence.title: LangGraph Streaming
evidence.quote: Use the messages streaming mode to stream Large Language Model (LLM) outputs token by token from any part of your graph

candidates.note: AppAgent runs an exploration phase (autonomous or learning from a human demo) that generates per-element documentation into a knowledge base, then a deployment phase that selects that documentation base to execute tasks.

evidence.title: AppAgent: Multimodal Agents as Smartphone Users
evidence.quote: The exploration phase starts with a task given by you, and you can choose to let the agent either explore the app on its own or learn from your demonstration. In both cases, the agent generates documentation for elements interacted during the exploration/demonstration and saves them for use in the deployment phase.

candidates.note: xLAM is a family of Large Action Models optimised for function calling and autonomous task execution rather than text generation, intended as the action engine of AI agents.

evidence.title: Salesforce/xLAM-7b-fc-r model card
evidence.quote: Large Action Models (LAMs) are advanced large language models designed to enhance decision-making and translate user intentions into executable actions that interact with the world. LAMs autonomously plan and execute tasks to achieve specific goals, serving as the brains of AI agents.

candidates.note: AxACE mirrors the ACE Generator-Reflector-Curator loop, holds context as a structured bullet playbook, and applies add/update/remove deltas (offline compile and online inference-time updates) to resist context collapse.

evidence.title: Agentic Context Engineering (ACE) - Ax
evidence.quote: AxACE is an optimizer that mirrors the ACE paper's Generator → Reflector → Curator loop. It represents context as structured bullets, applies incremental deltas, and returns a serialized playbook you can save, load, and keep updating at inference time.

evidence.title: ax-llm/ax index.ts (AxACE / AxACEOptimizedProgram exports)
evidence.quote: export { AxACE }; export { AxACEOptimizedProgram };

candidates.note: OPA decision logs mint a self-contained record at the moment each authorization decision executes, bundling the policy evaluated, the exact input data read, the decision result, and identifying metadata — a structured, exportable decision token per action; authorizing identity rides in input/metadata rather than as a first-class field, hence medium confidence.

evidence.title: Monitoring OPA — Decision Logs
evidence.quote: OPA can periodically report decision logs to remote HTTP servers, using custom plugins, or to the console output... The decision log includes the input data, the policy decision, and other metadata.

candidates.note: DeepEval is a dedicated testing harness whose sole job is running metric-based test cases against another LLM app's outputs, integrating with pytest so agent outputs are unit-tested like code; note its scope overlaps eval-harness (offline batch) rather than a true agent-evaluating-agent.

evidence.title: DeepEval — The Open-Source LLM Evaluation Framework
evidence.quote: DeepEval is an open-source evaluation framework for LLMs... it lets you "unit test" LLM outputs in a similar way to Pytest.

candidates.note: Agentforce manufactures agent instances from reusable agent templates that package topics, instructions, and actions, deployed via metadata so one template definition produces multiple consistent agents; registry-backed identities and atomic propagation are only partially documented, hence medium confidence.

evidence.title: Agentforce Agent Templates — Salesforce Help
evidence.quote: An agent template is a reusable definition of an agent... use a template to create multiple agents with the same topics and actions.

candidates.note: Arize evaluates intermediate agent artifacts — sending the ordered list of tool calls (the trajectory) to an LLM judge and scoring individual tool-calling spans — rather than only the final output, isolating mistakes an agent makes between steps.

evidence.title: Agent Trajectory Evaluations — Arize AX Docs
evidence.quote: Individual span or trace evaluations check that one step or response is correct, but they can miss costly mistakes an agent makes between steps.

evidence.title: Agent Trajectory Evaluations — Arize AX Docs
evidence.quote: Send the ordered list of tool calls to an LLM judge – Phoenix Evals classifies the trajectory as correct or incorrect.

candidates.note: Statsig Autotune is a multi-armed bandit that uses Thompson sampling to dynamically reallocate traffic toward better-performing variants in real time, minimizing regret rather than running a fixed-split A/B test; it is general-purpose experimentation, not agent/LLM-specific, hence medium confidence.

evidence.title: Autotune — Statsig Docs
evidence.quote: Autotune uses a multi-armed bandit approach... Statsig uses Thompson Sampling, a Bayesian inference technique, to allocate traffic to the best performing variants.

candidates.note: LangSmith provides offline experiments to gate during development and online evaluators that run automatically on production traces for real-time monitoring, feeding failing production traces back into offline experiments.

evidence.title: LangSmith Evaluation — Docs by LangChain
evidence.quote: Evaluators run automatically on runs or threads, providing real-time monitoring, anomaly detection, and alerting.

evidence.title: LangSmith Evaluation — Docs by LangChain
evidence.quote: Add failing production traces to your dataset, create targeted evaluators, validate fixes with offline experiments, and redeploy.

candidates.note: BAML makes every prompt a typed function defined in source files in the application repo, version-controlled with the code and testable in-editor, so prompts are owned and tested by the team rather than inherited as a framework default.

evidence.title: What is BAML? — BAML Documentation
evidence.quote: BAML is a domain-specific language to write and test LLM functions... Prompts are version controlled with your code.

candidates.note: NeMo Guardrails wraps the LLM in a programmable rails layer that checks and filters inputs/outputs around the model loop, relocating safety/correctness rigor into surrounding scaffolding rather than trusting the model; this is a guardrail-only facet of the broader pattern, hence medium confidence.

evidence.title: NVIDIA/NeMo-Guardrails
evidence.quote: NeMo Guardrails is an open-source toolkit for easily adding programmable guardrails to LLM-based conversational applications. Guardrails... are specific ways of controlling the output of a large language model.

candidates.note: Langfuse ingests full production traces and lets you attach LLM-as-a-judge evaluators that run on a configurable sampling percentage of traces so judge cost stays bounded as traffic grows.

evidence.title: LLM-as-a-Judge — Langfuse Docs
evidence.quote: Configure sampling percentage (e.g., 5%) to manage evaluation costs and throughput

candidates.note: Falco is a runtime security engine that parses kernel syscalls and emits alerts when a process violates its expected boundary — unexpected network egress, filesystem writes outside scope, or shell execution inside a container — exactly the sandbox-boundary-violation-as-telemetry mechanism, though it is general runtime security rather than agent-sandbox-specific.

evidence.title: Falco — Detect threats at runtime
evidence.quote: Falco uses system calls to secure and monitor a system, by: Parsing the Linux system calls from the kernel at runtime, Asserting the stream against a powerful rules engine, Alerting when a rule is violated.

evidence.title: Falco Rules — Default Rules
evidence.quote: A shell is run in a container... Unexpected outbound network connections... detect attempts to write to directories that are typically read-only.

candidates.note: Braintrust online scoring runs configured scorers against production logs automatically as they arrive, asynchronously in the background, observing and logging quality without adding latency or regenerating the output.

evidence.title: Score production traces — Braintrust Docs
evidence.quote: Online scoring evaluates production traces automatically as they're logged, running evaluations asynchronously in the background to provide continuous quality monitoring without affecting your application's latency or performance.

items.name: Three Layers of Agentic AI Memory

candidates.note: Cognee architects agent knowledge as integrated layers including a feedback layer (the memify step) that reweights graph edges from usage signals, treating memory as an evolving unit rather than static storage; the layer naming differs from the pattern's STM/LTM/feedback wording.

evidence.title: AI Agent Knowledge Base: The Three Layers That Actually Matter
evidence.quote: Feedback data is the quality signal—which recalls helped, which hallucinated, which resolutions stuck—and a knowledge base that doesn't capture this never improves; feedback is the layer most products skip

candidates.note: Each A2A server publishes an Agent Card JSON (identity, skills, service endpoint, auth) at the well-known URI /.well-known/agent-card.json so clients discover and bind to it by capability at runtime.

evidence.title: Agent2Agent (A2A) Protocol Specification
evidence.quote: Agent Card: A JSON metadata document published by an A2A Server, describing its identity, capabilities, skills, service endpoint, and authentication requirements.

evidence.title: Agent Discovery - A2A Protocol
evidence.quote: A2A Servers make their Agent Card discoverable by hosting it at a standardized, `well-known` URI on their domain. The standard path is `https://{agent-server-domain}/.well-known/agent-card.json`

candidates.note: A2A lets vendor- and framework-independent agents, each grounded in its own enterprise system, securely exchange information and coordinate actions across enterprise platforms via a standard inter-agent protocol.

evidence.title: Announcing the Agent2Agent Protocol (A2A)
evidence.quote: securely exchange information, and coordinate actions on top of various enterprise platforms or applications

evidence.title: Announcing the Agent2Agent Protocol (A2A)
evidence.quote: A2A empowers developers to build agents capable of connecting with any other agent built using the protocol

candidates.note: Sends a prompt to several different LLMs acting as proposers, then a final aggregator LLM synthesizes their outputs into one answer; realises the role-specialized-personas-on-different-architectures plus synthesis core, though the aggregator is not isolated to structured-JSON-only.

evidence.title: Together Mixture of Agents (MoA)
evidence.quote: given a prompt, like `tell me the best things to do in SF`, it sends it to 4 different OSS LLMs. It then combines results from all 4, sends it to a final LLM, and asks it to combine all 4 responses into an ideal response

candidates.note: Permission modes form a staged autonomy ladder (default review-each-action through looser modes) that the user widens as trust in the agent's direction grows, with sensitive work kept under more oversight.

evidence.title: Choose a permission mode - Claude Code Docs
evidence.quote: The mode you pick shapes the flow of a session: default mode has you review each action as it comes, while looser modes let Claude work in longer uninterrupted stretches and report back when done. Pick more oversight for sensitive work, or fewer interruptions when you trust the direction.

candidates.note: Durable agent workflows use saga patterns and distributed compensation to roll back across agents/systems when steps that span services partially fail.

evidence.title: Building AI agents that overcome the complexity cliff
evidence.quote: Cross-system signaling, saga patterns, distributed compensation

candidates.note: Splits the agent into a fast Talker that synthesizes conversational replies and a slow Reasoner that handles multi-step planning and tool calls, so chat never blocks on reasoning.

evidence.title: Agents Thinking Fast and Slow: A Talker-Reasoner Architecture (arXiv:2410.08328)
evidence.quote: slower, more deliberative, and more logical, and is tasked with multi-step reasoning and planning, calling tools, performing actions

candidates.note: Beyond delegation, the manager acts as an explicit observer: it evaluates each worker's outcome against required standards before the result is accepted, instead of trusting it blindly.

evidence.title: Hierarchical Process - CrewAI
evidence.quote: The manager evaluates outcomes to ensure they meet the required standards.

candidates.note: Parlant replaces free-form chain-of-thought with Attentive Reasoning Queries — structured JSON reasoning blueprints whose targeted queries re-anchor the model's attention to critical guidelines and prior decisions at the generation steps where adherence slips; it runs distinct ARQ sets per component (guideline matching, tool-calling, message composition).

evidence.title: Enforcement & Explainability — Parlant Docs
evidence.quote: Attentive Reasoning Queries (ARQs) are essentially structured reasoning blueprints built into prompts that guide LLMs through specific thinking patterns

evidence.title: Enforcement & Explainability — Parlant Docs
evidence.quote: Parlant uses different sets of ARQs for each of its components (e.g., guideline matching, tool-calling, or message composition)

candidates.note: DeepSeek bootstraps reasoning from the model's own chain-of-thought without human reasoning labels — R1-Zero learns CoT via pure RL with no SFT, and the R1 pipeline then fine-tunes on self-generated, reward-filtered reasoning traces. (RL/GRPO-based bootstrap, close to but not the literal STaR algorithm.)

evidence.title: DeepSeek-R1 — model card
evidence.quote: We directly apply reinforcement learning (RL) to the base model without relying on supervised fine-tuning (SFT) as a preliminary step. This approach allows the model to explore chain-of-thought (CoT)

evidence.title: DeepSeek-R1 — model card
evidence.quote: The pipeline incorporates two RL stages aimed at discovering improved reasoning patterns and aligning with human preferences, as well as two SFT stages

candidates.framework: Azure AI Foundry RAFT fine-tuning recipe
candidates.note: An official Azure recipe that generates synthetic Q-doc-answer triplets with oracle and distractor documents and fine-tunes a model on Azure AI Foundry to ignore the distractors in domain-specific RAG.

evidence.title: Azure-Samples/azureai-foundry-finetuning-raft
evidence.quote: This repository is a recipe that will walk you through improving RAG system precision using UC Berkeley's RAFT technique on Azure AI Foundry.

evidence.title: RAFT: Sailing Llama towards better domain-specific RAG (Meta / Microsoft)
evidence.quote: These 'distractor' documents may be pulled into the model's context even when they are not good sources for a well-reasoned answer.

candidates.note: A Python/Rust differential-dataflow framework that processes documents into RAG features incrementally as a continuous stream and keeps a live vector index synchronised with sources in real time, no batch re-indexing.

evidence.title: Why Pathway
evidence.quote: real-time data indexes allowing you to effortlessly synchronize your index with data sources in real time

candidates.note: BPMN is the deterministic spine: the LLM agent runs inside a BPMN ad-hoc sub-process whose nodes define the available tools/steps. The model only decides which tool to call next; Zeebe deterministically executes the selected BPMN activities, stores state, applies retries/incident handling. Decision and orchestration are intentionally split.

evidence.title: AI agents | Camunda 8 Docs
evidence.quote: LLM decides: Which tool to call next, in what order, and with which parameters. Camunda orchestrates: Executes the selected BPMN activity, stores variables, applies retries and incident handling, and routes human tasks and events.

evidence.title: Enterprise-Grade Agentic Automation Is Here
evidence.quote: Having both deterministic and dynamic flows run in a single engine enables you to handle the entire spectrum of business processes: the straightforward, pre-defined work that automation excels at—along with this new, agentic way of working that thrives on non-linear work based on context.

candidates.note: Per query, Semantic Router takes the fast symbolic/vector path — it decides the route via embedding similarity in semantic vector space instead of calling the slow neural LLM. Queries that match a defined route are dispatched deterministically; only unmatched inputs fall through to LLM generation, giving an explicit per-query split between a cheap symbolic layer and the neural model.

evidence.title: aurelio-labs/semantic-router README
evidence.quote: Semantic Router is a superfast decision-making layer for your LLMs and agents. Rather than waiting for slow LLM generations to make tool-use decisions, we use the magic of semantic vector space to make those decisions — routing our requests using semantic meaning.

candidates.note: Portkey's AI Gateway routes each request to a provider target via symbolic rules over metadata and request params (user plan, model flag, region, temperature) — a deterministic rule path sitting in front of the neural models. Secondary/weaker match: it is rule-based dispatch, not a knowledge-graph symbolic reasoner.

evidence.title: Conditional Routing - Portkey Docs
evidence.quote: Using Portkey Gateway, you can route your requests to different provider targets based on custom conditions you define... Metadata - Custom key-value pairs you pass with your requests; Request Parameters - Any parameter that you send in your original LLM request.

candidates.framework: Design Patterns for Securing LLM Agents (Action-Selector)
candidates.vendor: Beurer-Kellner et al. (Invariant Labs / ETH Zurich / Google DeepMind / Microsoft)
candidates.note: The agent acts only as an action selector that translates a request into one of a fixed set of predefined tool calls, with no feedback path from tool outputs back into its reasoning.

evidence.title: Design Patterns for Securing LLM Agents against Prompt Injections
evidence.quote: A relatively simple pattern that makes agents immune to prompt injections — while still allowing them to take external actions — is to prevent any feedback from these actions back into the agent.

candidates.note: A single permission-mode parameter (plan, default, acceptEdits, auto, dontAsk, bypassPermissions) spans read-only assistant to fully autonomous worker and is switchable mid-session, at startup, or as a default without changing the codebase.

evidence.title: Choose a permission mode - Claude Code Docs
evidence.quote: Permission modes control how often that pause happens. The mode you pick shapes the flow of a session: default mode has you review each action as it comes, while looser modes let Claude work in longer uninterrupted stretches and report back when done.

evidence.title: Switch permission modes - Claude Code Docs
evidence.quote: You can switch modes mid-session, at startup, or as a persistent default.

candidates.note: The privileged LLM emits code that a custom Python interpreter executes while tracking capabilities on every value, enforcing per-value security policies on data flows when tools are called.

evidence.title: Defeating Prompt Injections by Design (CaMeL, arXiv:2503.18813)
evidence.quote: To further improve security, CaMeL uses a notion of a capability to prevent the exfiltration of private data over unauthorized data flows by enforcing security policies when tools are called.

candidates.note: Validators such as ValidChoices (allow-listed enums) and ValidLength (max lengths) compose into Input Guards that intercept and constrain untrusted LLM inputs to a strictly formatted, typed interface.

evidence.title: Validators — Guardrails AI
evidence.quote: Validators can be combined together into Input and Output Guards that intercept the inputs and outputs of LLMs.

evidence.title: guardrails-ai/valid_choice
evidence.quote: Guardrails AI: Valid choices - validates that a value is within the acceptable choices

candidates.note: CaMeL extracts the control and data flow from the trusted user query up front, so untrusted tool outputs and retrieved data can never redirect the agent's planned step sequence at runtime.

evidence.title: Defeating Prompt Injections by Design (CaMeL, arXiv:2503.18813)
evidence.quote: CaMeL explicitly extracts the control and data flows from the (trusted) query; therefore, the untrusted data retrieved by the LLM can never impact the program flow.

candidates.note: LangGraph's recursion_limit caps how many steps a graph can run before raising GraphRecursionError, stopping a runaway loop — a step-count guard rather than content-similarity detection of near-duplicate outputs.

evidence.title: GRAPH_RECURSION_LIMIT — Docs by LangChain
evidence.quote: Your LangGraph `StateGraph` reached the maximum number of steps before hitting a stop condition.

candidates.note: Splits work between a privileged planner that extracts control/data flow from the trusted query and a quarantined model that parses untrusted data into typed references, so untrusted content can never drive the program flow.

evidence.title: Defeating Prompt Injections by Design (CaMeL) — abstract
evidence.quote: To operate, CaMeL explicitly extracts the control and data flows from the (trusted) query; therefore, the untrusted data retrieved by the LLM can never impact the program flow.

evidence.title: Defeating Prompt Injections by Design (CaMeL) — capabilities
evidence.quote: To further improve security, CaMeL uses a notion of a capability to prevent the exfiltration of private data over unauthorized data flows by enforcing security policies when tools are called.

candidates.note: Defines a Privileged LLM that holds tool access and a Quarantined LLM that handles untrusted content with no tools, passing only unique tokens that reference tainted content between them.

evidence.title: The Dual LLM pattern for building AI assistants that can resist prompt injection
evidence.quote: The Quarantined LLM is used any time we need to work with untrusted content—content that might conceivably incorporate a prompt injection attack. It does not have access to tools

evidence.title: The Dual LLM pattern — opaque references
evidence.quote: Instead of forwarding the text as-is, we can instead work with unique tokens that represent that potentially tainted content.

candidates.note: Output rails run after generation and can modify the bot response programmatically; a Colang output-rail subflow rewrites $bot_message outside the model's discretion — the mechanism for attaching a non-suppressible disclaimer.

evidence.title: Configuration Reference - Rail Types (Output rails)
evidence.quote: Validate, filter, or modify bot responses

evidence.title: Sample config.co - output rail subflow appending to bot message
evidence.quote:
define subflow dummy output rail
  $bot_message = $bot_message + "!!"

candidates.note: Khanmigo's tutor deliberately withholds the direct answer and instead walks the learner through the solution with questions and step-by-step hints until they work it out themselves.

evidence.title: Meet Khanmigo: Khan Academy's AI-powered teaching assistant and tutor
evidence.quote: Khanmigo doesn't just give answers. Instead, with limitless patience, it guides learners to find the answer themselves.

evidence.title: Khanmigo for learners: Always-available tutor, powered by AI
evidence.quote: Unlike ChatGPT, Khanmigo never gives you the answer. It's built to help you learn.

candidates.note: Names the threat model directly: an agent is exploitable only when it simultaneously holds access to private data, exposure to untrusted content, and an external communication channel, so removing any one leg blocks exfiltration.

evidence.title: The lethal trifecta for AI agents
evidence.quote: If your agent combines these three features, an attacker can easily trick it into accessing your private data and sending it to that attacker.

evidence.title: The lethal trifecta for AI agents — the three legs
evidence.quote: Exposure to untrusted content—any mechanism by which text (or images) controlled by a malicious attacker could become available to your LLM

candidates.framework: Design Patterns for Securing LLM Agents (LLM Map-Reduce)
candidates.vendor: Beurer-Kellner et al. (Invariant Labs / ETH Zurich / Google DeepMind / Microsoft)
candidates.note: Each untrusted document is dispatched to its own isolated sub-agent (the map step) and only the safely aggregated results are merged, so an injection in one document cannot steer the processing of another.

evidence.title: Design Patterns for Securing LLM Agents against Prompt Injections (arXiv:2506.08837v2)
evidence.quote: Untrusted documents are processed independently, to ensure that a malicious document cannot impact the processing of another document.

candidates.note: A natively multimodal safety classifier evaluates mixed text-and-image prompts and responses together, classifying each as safe or unsafe across modalities rather than text only.

evidence.title: meta-llama/Llama-Guard-4-12B model card
evidence.quote: Llama Guard 4 is a natively multimodal safety classifier with 12 billion parameters trained jointly on text and multiple images.

evidence.title: meta-llama/Llama-Guard-4-12B model card
evidence.quote: Unlike Llama Guard 3-11B-vision, Llama Guard 4 now supports safety classification when multiple images are given in the prompt as input.

candidates.note: Applies the same content-filter policy categories to image content as to text, blocking harmful multimodal content across hate, insults, sexual, violence, misconduct, and prompt-attack categories.

evidence.title: Amazon Bedrock Guardrails announces the general availability of industry-leading image content filters
evidence.quote: Image content filters can be applied to all categories within the content filter policy of Bedrock Guardrails including hate, insults, sexual, violence, misconduct, and prompt attack.

candidates.note: An MCP server acts as a policy enforcement point that calls the Cerbos policy decision point on every AI-agent tool invocation, returning an allow/deny decision so only the actions permitted for that user and context execute.

evidence.title: MCP Permissions. Securing AI Agent Access to Tools. | Cerbos
evidence.quote: The MCP server simply becomes a policy enforcement point: it asks Cerbos for a decision and abides by it.

evidence.title: Enterprise-Grade Authorization for MCP Servers | Cerbos
evidence.quote: When clients connect, your MCP server calls Cerbos to check which tools are permitted for that user and context, then enables/disables tools accordingly.

candidates.note: A human-authored chain of command assigns each instruction source a default authority level (Platform/System > Developer > User > Guideline) and resolves conflicts deterministically by that ordering rather than leaving precedence to model discretion.

evidence.title: OpenAI Model Spec (2025/09/12) - Chain of command
evidence.quote: Each section of the spec, and message role in the input conversation, is designated with a default authority level

evidence.title: OpenAI Model Spec (2025/09/12) - Chain of command
evidence.quote: Instructions with higher authority override those with lower authority.

candidates.note: Ramp's Accounting Agent auto-codes and auto-syncs routine, in-policy spend on its own, but routes lower-confidence and exception transactions to a human and posts nothing to the ERP without explicit confirmation, gating material postings on a human principal.

evidence.title: 12 Best AI Accounting Software Tools for 2026
evidence.quote: Routine in-policy spend moves from swipe to ERP automatically, and only exceptions surface for your review.

evidence.title: 12 Best AI Accounting Software Tools for 2026
evidence.quote: nothing posts to your ERP without your confirmation

candidates.note: Topical/dialog rails restrict the bot to its purpose and enforce code-defined Colang flows (user ask about X -> bot refuse to respond about X) that block off-topic professional advice such as investing advice.

evidence.title: Topical Rails tutorial (NeMo Guardrails docs)
evidence.quote: Topical rails keep the bot talking only about the topics related to its purpose. In the case of the ABC Bot, for example, it should not talk about cooking or giving investing advice.

evidence.title: Topical Rails tutorial - disallowed-topics refusal flow
evidence.quote:
define flow
  user ask about cooking
  bot refuse to respond about cooking

candidates.note: The agent runs every infrastructure operation in a dry-run mode that shows exactly what would be created/modified/deleted and presents the execution plan for human approval before any real AWS changes are applied.

evidence.title: VersusControl/ai-infrastructure-agent README
evidence.quote: All operations can be run in 'dry-run' mode first: Shows exactly what would be created/modified/deleted

candidates.note: Ollama downloads and runs model weights and inference entirely on the operator's own machine and exposes a local API, so prompts and outputs never cross into a third-party API.

evidence.title: Ollama FAQ
evidence.quote: Ollama runs locally. We don't see your prompts or data when you run locally.

candidates.vendor: vLLM Project (originally UC Berkeley Sky Computing Lab)
candidates.note: vLLM is an open-source LLM inference and serving engine the operator deploys on its own GPUs/CPUs, keeping the entire inference stack inside infrastructure the operator controls.

evidence.title: vllm-project/vllm README — Hardware support
evidence.quote: Support for NVIDIA GPUs, AMD GPUs, and x86/ARM/PowerPC CPUs. Additionally, diverse hardware plugins such as Google TPUs, Intel Gaudi, IBM Spyre, Huawei Ascend, Rebellions NPU, Apple Silicon, MetaX GPU, and more.

candidates.note: Output rails run programmable deterministic checks on the LLM's generated output and can reject it (blocking delivery) or alter it before it reaches the user or any downstream side-effect.

evidence.title: Guardrails Process — NeMo Guardrails docs source
evidence.quote: Output rails: applied to the output generated by the LLM; an output rail can reject the output, preventing it from being returned to the user or alter it (e.g., removing sensitive data).

candidates.note: PromptGuard scans untrusted content such as web and tool data while AlignmentCheck reasons over the agent's trace to catch goal hijacking from misleading tool output and indirect prompt injection before the agent acts on it.

evidence.title: PurpleLlama/LlamaFirewall README — AlignmentCheck
evidence.quote: It uses few-shot prompting and semantic analysis to detect goal hijacking, indirect prompt injections, and signs of agent misalignment.

evidence.title: AlignmentCheck | LlamaFirewall
evidence.quote: This helps detect and prevent covert prompt injection, misleading tool output, and other forms of goal hijacking.

candidates.note: Documents that MCP tool descriptions and output cannot be trusted and prescribes version pinning plus its Guardrails/MCP-scan tooling to constrain untrusted tool content.

evidence.title: MCP Security Notification: Tool Poisoning Attacks
evidence.quote: A Tool Poisoning Attack occurs when malicious instructions are embedded within MCP tool descriptions that are invisible to users but visible to AI models.

evidence.title: MCP Security Notification: Tool Poisoning Attacks
evidence.quote: Clients should pin the version of the MCP server and its tools to prevent unauthorized changes.

candidates.note: Defines a single fixed taxonomy of machine-readable hazard codes (S1-S14, aligned to the MLCommons taxonomy) that the guard model emits as the refusal/block reason, so unsafe classifications are triaged by code rather than by parsing human-readable text.

evidence.title: Llama Guard 3-8B Model Card
evidence.quote: The model is trained to predict safety labels on the 14 categories shown below, based on the MLCommons taxonomy of 13 hazards, as well as an additional category for Code Interpreter Abuse for tool calls use cases.

evidence.title: Llama Guard 3-8B Model Card - Hazard categories
evidence.quote: S1: Violent Crimes S2: Non-Violent Crimes S3: Sex-Related Crimes S4: Child Sexual Exploitation S5: Defamation S6: Specialized Advice S7: Privacy S8: Intellectual Property S9: Indiscriminate Weapons S10: Hate S11: Suicide & Self-Harm S12: Sexual Content S13: Elections S14: Code Interpreter Abuse

candidates.note: Anchord's MCP server makes agents resolve companies and people to a canonical AnchorID against its identity graph (system of record) instead of emitting identifiers themselves, returning needs_review on ambiguity.

evidence.title: Anchord MCP (Glama listing)
evidence.quote: Resolve companies and people to canonical AnchorIDs, run pre-write safety checks, and export golden records — through the standard MCP tool interface.

candidates.note: Stainless's MCP architecture guidance prescribes composite tools that perform a sequence of API calls as a single higher-level tool for multi-step workflows.

evidence.title: API MCP Server Architecture Guide for API Providers
evidence.quote: Composite tools: Create higher-level tools that perform a sequence of API calls.

candidates.note: Azure API Management exposes a managed REST API as an MCP server by turning selected API operations one-to-one into agent-callable MCP tools.

evidence.title: Expose REST API as MCP server - Azure API Management
evidence.quote: Expose one or more of the API operations as tools that MCP clients can call by using the MCP protocol. ... The portal creates the MCP server and exposes the API operations as tools.

candidates.note: Agno agents consume any external MCP server as tools via MCPTools, and AgentOS can be flipped to an MCP server (enable_mcp_server=True) that publishes its agents, teams, and workflows back to MCP clients.

evidence.title: Model Context Protocol - Agno
evidence.quote: You can connect your Agents to any MCP server, using Agno's MCP integration.

evidence.title: AgentOS as MCP Server - Agno
evidence.quote: By exposing your AgentOS as an MCP server, external clients that can handle MCP-compatible applications will be able to connect to your AgentOS and interact with it.

candidates.note: Skyvern 2.0 splits its browser agent into separate Planner, Actor, and Validator roles: the planner decomposes goals, the actor grounds and fires clicks, and the validator re-reads the screenshot to judge completion and loop back on failure.

evidence.title: Skyvern 2.0: State-of-the-Art Web Navigation with 85.8% on WebVoyager
evidence.quote: This acts as a supervisor function to confirm that the Task executor is achieving its objectives as expected, and report any errors / tweaks back to the Planner

candidates.note: Cube routes AI-agent data questions through a governed semantic layer so agents query vetted metrics and dimensions over SQL/REST/GraphQL/MCP rather than free-authoring raw SQL against the warehouse.

evidence.title: Best Semantic Layer for AI and BI (2026)
evidence.quote: the semantic layer is the foundation of an AI-native platform — SQL-first, extensible at query time, and reachable over SQL, REST, GraphQL, and MCP so AI agents query governed metrics instead of raw tables.

candidates.note: Composio maintains a catalog of 1000+ toolkits with semantic tool search so an agent can query the registry by use case and retrieve the right tools instead of loading them all.

evidence.title: ComposioHQ/composio (README)
evidence.quote: Composio powers 1000+ toolkits, tool search, context management, authentication, and a sandboxed workbench to help you build AI agents that turn intent into action.

evidence.title: Fetching tools and schemas - Composio Docs
evidence.quote: Find tools semantically.

candidates.note: Arcade exposes a registry/catalog of thousands of prebuilt MCP tools that agents browse and pick from, plus a registry to share and discover tools.

evidence.title: Arcade home
evidence.quote: Browse thousands of pre-built MCP tools for the apps your org already runs on.

candidates.note: Its model_graded_qa() scorer runs a separate grader model whose prompt template exposes only question, answer, criterion and instructions, so the grader judges the answer against the rubric without ever seeing the producing model's reasoning trace.

evidence.title: inspect_ai.scorer — model_graded_qa reference
evidence.quote: This template has four variables: question, criterion, answer, and instructions (which is fed from the instructions parameter). ... Model or models to use for grading. If a list is provided, each model grades independently and the final grade is computed by majority vote.

candidates.note: Enabling openai_logprobs surfaces per-token log probabilities on the model response (ModelResponse.provider_details['logprobs']), the raw signal callers use to attach a confidence value to the answer.

evidence.title: Pydantic AI — OpenAI model API reference (logprobs)
evidence.quote: Include log probabilities in the response. For Chat models, these will be included in `ModelResponse.provider_details['logprobs']`.

candidates.note: Combines validators into Input Guards and Output Guards that intercept and check the inputs and outputs on both sides of the LLM call.

evidence.title: guardrails-ai/guardrails README
evidence.quote: Guardrails runs Input/Output Guards in your application that detect, quantify and mitigate the presence of specific types of risks.

evidence.title: Introduction - Guardrails AI
evidence.quote: Multiple validators can be combined together into Input and Output Guards that intercept the inputs and outputs of LLMs.

candidates.note: Ragas rejects naive LLM prompting (which mode-collapses) and instead enumerates named question evolution types (simple, reasoning, multi_context, conditional) with a configurable distribution plus named personas to seed test generation across defined dimensions.

evidence.title: Synthetic Test Data generation | Ragas
evidence.quote: LLMs by default are not good at creating diverse samples as it tends to follow common paths. ... questions with different characteristics such as reasoning, conditioning, multi-context, and more are systematically crafted from the provided set of documents.

evidence.title: Persona Generation | Ragas
evidence.quote: You can add different persona's to the testset generation process by defining the Persona class with the name and role description ... you can use these personas in the testset generation process by passing them to the TestsetGenerator class.

candidates.note: G-Eval is an LLM-as-judge metric where you author fixed evaluation criteria up front and attach an explicit Rubric to confine the judge to predefined score ranges, so the reviewer grades against your hand-authored criteria rather than inventing new ones.

evidence.title: G-Eval | DeepEval — The LLM Evaluation Framework
evidence.quote: G-Eval is a framework that uses LLM-as-a-judge with chain-of-thoughts (CoT) to evaluate LLM outputs based on ANY custom criteria. ... You can provide a list of Rubrics through the rubric argument to confine your evaluation LLM to output in specific score ranges.

candidates.note: The plan-and-execute agent has a planner that emits a multi-step plan, executor(s) that run each step with tools, and a re-plan step that re-evaluates results against the objective to decide whether to finish or continue.

evidence.title: Plan-and-Execute Agents (LangChain blog)
evidence.quote: A planner, which prompts an LLM to generate a multi-step plan to complete a large task. Executor(s), which accept the user query and a step in the plan and invoke 1 or more tools to complete that task.

evidence.title: Plan-and-Execute Agents — re-planning step
evidence.quote: Once execution is completed, the agent is called again with a re-planning prompt, letting it decide whether to finish with a response or whether to generate a follow-up plan

candidates.note: Generates adversarial / red-team test cases and runs them in CI as a gate, exiting non-zero to fail the build when a safety or security assertion regresses before deployment.

evidence.title: LLM red teaming guide (open source)
evidence.quote: Many organizations build this into their development cycle and into processes like CI/CD.

evidence.title: CI/CD Integration for LLM Eval and Security
evidence.quote: Catch regressions early - Test prompt changes before they reach production. Quality gates - Enforce minimum performance thresholds.

candidates.note: Citations are parsed and resolved against the provided source documents so every returned cited_text is a valid pointer into a real source, preventing fabricated references.

evidence.title: Citations - Claude API Docs
evidence.quote: Better citation reliability: Because citations are parsed into the respective response formats mentioned above and cited_text is extracted, citations are guaranteed to contain valid pointers to the provided documents.

candidates.vendor: Jason / AgentSpeak community (R. Bordini, J. Hubner et al.)
candidates.note: Jason is a maintained interpreter for AgentSpeak, the canonical BDI agent language: agents hold an explicit belief base, adopt desires/goals, and commit to intentions (plans), reasoning by reconciling the three in the operational semantics of the language. This is the direct, named implementation of the BDI architecture rather than an LLM-era reinterpretation.

evidence.title: Jason, a BDI agent programming language
evidence.quote: Jason is an interpreter for an extended version of AgentSpeak. It implements the operational semantics of that language

evidence.title: BDI architecture (Jason / AgentSpeak overview)
evidence.quote: The BDI architecture models behavior based on the agent's Beliefs about the world, Desires representing its goals and Intentions, the plans it commits to in order to achieve those goals.

candidates.note: Promptfoo runs declarative assertion-based eval suites that return pass/fail and exit non-zero in CI, so a release is blocked unless the evals pass — the eval suite literally is the release gate.

evidence.title: Testing prompts with Promptfoo / CI/CD
evidence.quote: promptfoo can be used in CI/CD pipelines to automatically evaluate prompts, models, and RAG systems whenever changes are made... The eval command returns a non-zero exit code if any test fails.

candidates.note: Langfuse Prompt Management stores each prompt in a registry where every save creates a new immutable version number, and labels (production/staging) point at a chosen version so deploys and rollbacks are done by moving the label between versions.

evidence.title: Prompt Management — Langfuse Docs
evidence.quote: Version Control: Track the changes through versioning... Each prompt has a version number that increments with each change.

evidence.title: Prompt Management — Langfuse Docs
evidence.quote: Use labels to deploy prompt versions... The production label is used to fetch the prompt in production.

candidates.note: SageMaker shadow testing deploys a challenger model version alongside the production champion, mirroring a portion of real inference requests to the shadow variant and comparing outputs/metrics without ever returning shadow responses to users.

evidence.title: Shadow tests — Amazon SageMaker AI Developer Guide
evidence.quote: Amazon SageMaker AI helps you run shadow tests to evaluate a new machine learning (ML) model before production release by testing its performance against the currently deployed model.

evidence.title: Shadow tests — Amazon SageMaker AI Developer Guide
evidence.quote: deploy a new version of the model to the shadow fleet and route a portion of the inference requests to it. The model in the shadow fleet doesn't return any responses to the requests.

candidates.note: LangGraph exposes the run state as a writable scratchpad where an agent persists intermediate notes at any step and selectively re-exposes them to the model on later turns without forcing them into the response.

evidence.title: Persistence - Docs by LangChain
evidence.quote: Checkpointers persist a thread's graph state as checkpoints. Use them for short-term, thread-scoped memory, including conversation continuity, human-in-the-loop workflows, time travel, and fault tolerance.

evidence.title: Context Engineering for Agents - LangChain Blog
evidence.quote: The LeadResearcher begins by thinking through the approach and saving its plan to Memory to persist the context

candidates.note: JADE ships first-class ContractNetInitiator and ContractNetResponder behaviour classes implementing the FIPA Contract Net protocol: a manager solicits proposals via a CFP, responders propose, and the initiator awards via accept-proposal.

evidence.title: ContractNetResponder (JADE API)
evidence.quote: This behaviour implements the `fipa-contract-net` interaction protocol from the point of view of a responder to a call for proposal (`cfp`) message.

candidates.note: Generates multiple candidate thoughts/next-steps at each node, evaluates and prunes them, and uses BFS/DFS-style search (with LATS adding MCTS + backtracking) to select the best continuation.

evidence.title: Reflection Agents (LATS / Tree of Thoughts in LangGraph)
evidence.quote: Language Agent Tree Search (LATS) ... combines reflection/evaluation and search (specifically Monte-Carlo trees search) ... expansion with parallel action generation, reflection and evaluation of outcomes with scoring.

candidates.note: Uses change-data-capture so the source database stays the single writer; every insert/update/delete is streamed in real time and triggers an embedding update that keeps the vector store in sync.

evidence.title: Streaming CDC Events to Vector Databases for Real-Time AI - Streamkap
evidence.quote: use CDC to stream database changes to your vector database in real time. Every insert, update, and delete in the source triggers an embedding update

candidates.note: An Analyzer detects PII entities via NER/regex/rule-based recognizers and an Anonymizer redacts, masks, hashes, or encrypts them in text and images before or after model use.

evidence.title: microsoft/presidio — Context aware, pluggable PII de-identification
evidence.quote: Context aware, pluggable and customizable PII de-identification service for text and images.

evidence.title: microsoft/presidio — README capabilities
evidence.quote: It provides fast identification and anonymization modules for private entities in text such as credit card numbers, names, locations, social security numbers, bitcoin wallets, US phone numbers, financial data and more.

candidates.note: Sensitive information filters detect PII in input prompts and model responses and either block the content or mask/anonymize it by replacing each entity with its PII type.

evidence.title: Remove PII from conversations by using sensitive information filters - Amazon Bedrock
evidence.quote: Amazon Bedrock Guardrails helps detect sensitive information, such as personally identifiable information (PII), in input prompts or model responses using sensitive information filters.

evidence.title: Sensitive information filters — Mask mode
evidence.quote: Sensitive information filter policies can anonymize or redact information from model requests or responses. For example, guardrails mask PIIs while generating summaries of conversations between users and customer service agents.

candidates.note: A safety classifier evaluates each prompt and response against a hazard taxonomy and emits safe/unsafe plus the violated categories, giving the host application the signal to refuse out-of-policy requests.

evidence.title: meta-llama/Llama-Guard-3-8B model card
evidence.quote: it generates text in its output that indicates whether a given prompt or response is safe or unsafe, and if unsafe, it also lists the content categories violated.

evidence.title: meta-llama/Llama-Guard-3-8B model card
evidence.quote: can be used to classify content in both LLM inputs (prompt classification) and in LLM responses (response classification).

candidates.note: Programmable rails control LLM output to refuse disallowed subjects and respond in prescribed ways to specific user requests, keeping the assistant on its permitted topics.

evidence.title: NVIDIA-NeMo/Guardrails — README
evidence.quote: Guardrails (or "rails" for short) are specific ways of controlling the output of a large language model, such as not talking about politics, responding in a particular way to specific user requests, following a predefined dialog path, using a particular language style, and more.

evidence.title: NVIDIA-NeMo/Guardrails — README (domain-specific assistants)
evidence.quote: Domain-specific Assistants (a.k.a. chatbots): Ensure the assistant stays on topic and follows the designed conversational flows.

candidates.note: BAML lets a typed LLM function declare a tagged-union return type (e.g. -> StopTool | ReplyTool) so one function produces a family of related record shapes; its Schema-Aligned Parsing coerces the model output into whichever member variant was emitted.

evidence.title: BoundaryML/baml README
evidence.quote: We created the SAP (schema-aligned parsing) algorithm to support the flexible outputs LLMs can provide.

candidates.note: Promptfoo runs multiple prompt variants against a shared dataset in a matrix and scores outputs automatically; its model-graded select-best assertion then picks the winning variant by measurement rather than manual judgement.

evidence.title: Intro | Promptfoo
evidence.quote: promptfoo produces matrix views that let you quickly evaluate outputs across many prompts.

evidence.title: Select Best | Promptfoo
evidence.quote: Takes all outputs from the test case. Evaluates each output against the specified criterion. Selects the best output. ... This is useful for comparing different prompt or model variations to determine which produces the best result.

candidates.note: Toolformer is Meta AI's model trained in a self-supervised way to decide which APIs to call, when, and with what arguments, requiring only a handful of demonstrations per API and no human annotation.

evidence.title: Toolformer: Language Models Can Teach Themselves to Use Tools
evidence.quote: This is done in a self-supervised way, requiring nothing more than a handful of demonstrations for each API.

items.name: Blocking Sync Calls in Agent Loop
Race Conditions on Shared Tool Resources
Static Role for a Dynamic Agent

authoring_candidates.name: Agent Framework Bloat
authoring_candidates.summary: Stacking heavy abstractions above the model call (chains, runnables, callbacks, executors) that obscure the underlying tool-call loop and make behaviour hard to trace or debug. The corrective is to keep the loop close to raw code with minimal abstraction layers.

raised_by.note: smolagents states simplicity as an explicit design value and deliberately designs against framework bloat: agent logic fits in ~1k LoC with minimal abstractions above raw code. Re-add this to smolagents.anti_patterns_avoided once the anti-pattern is authored.

evidence.title: smolagents — index, simplicity
evidence.quote: Simplicity: The logic for agents fits in ~thousand lines of code. We kept abstractions to their minimal shape above raw code!
